scenario_name: Scenario 14 — Anti-Pattern Detection in Existing Architecture
justification: All 5 anti-patterns identified by name: shared database (Order/Courier), synchronous Notification fan-in, god endpoint (/api/v1/everything), REST-based background jobs, and direct cross-service DB reads (Reporting→Order). Remediation is clearly prioritized into 4 phases by risk severity — shared database and direct DB reads are Phase 1-2 (highest priority), REST jobs Phase 3, Notification fan-in Phase 3, god endpoint Phase 4. REST-based jobs explicitly called out as fragile (publicly routable, no retry semantics, no concurrency control). Trade-offs for shared database split are detailed (data migration, dual-write period, eventual consistency, infrastructure cost). Incremental strangler-fig approach explicitly recommended with pitfall warning against big-bang rewrite.

observations.text: All 5 anti-patterns identified with clear explanations of why each is harmful
Prioritization is excellent — 4-phase plan ordered by blast radius, with shared DB and direct DB reads as highest priority
Trade-offs section is thorough, covering each remediation decision with gains, sacrifices, and alternatives considered
Explicitly warns against big-bang rewrite and recommends strangler fig pattern
REST-as-job-trigger anti-pattern correctly identified with security implications (publicly routable endpoints)
Domain model and context map provide DDD-grounded analysis of boundary violations
Immediate tactical mitigations (circuit breakers, permissions, auth) provided alongside strategic fixes
Phase timeline estimates (weeks 1-18) are speculative without knowing team size or codebase complexity — could set unrealistic expectations

agent_output_excerpt:
{
  "executive_summary": "This system exhibits five distinct architectural anti-patterns accumulated over 3 years of organic growth. The most critical are the shared database between Order and Courier services (defeating service autonomy), and the Reporting Service's direct database reads (creating hidden coupling).
